summary.intent: Complete a PR (#713) adding a TagGroup component by addressing Codex review comments, getting CI green, and obtaining final approval.
summary.outcome: All Codex review threads were resolved and approval obtained; Coverage CI job was unblocked by refreshing the stale .crap-baseline.json using the CI's own lcov.info artifact after normalizing runner paths.

repo: The repo uses a CRAP regression gate (cargo xtask ci crap / cargo xcrap --update-baseline) that compares against a committed .crap-baseline.json; when main advances with new code, the baseline drifts and the gate fails on unrelated functions.

CI uploads a 'coverage-lcov' artifact containing lcov.info with absolute runner paths (/home/runner/work/ars-ui/ars-ui/); these must be rewritten to repo-relative paths (SF:./) before running local baseline tools.
The Coverage CI job failing while Codecov itself shows green is a signal of a CRAP threshold/baseline failure, not a coverage percentage regression.
The project uses cargo xtask for CI tasks (e.g., cargo xtask ci crap) and xcrap subcommand for baseline management.
Spec documentation for components lives in spec/components/data-display/<component>.md alongside the implementation.

code.finding: Must be refreshed whenever main advances with new code that adds functions; stale baseline causes broad false CRAP regressions on the PR's merge-ref CI run.
TagGroup component implementation; was subject to a Codex stale-focus review thread that was resolved by a targeted fix and push.

workflow: When a Coverage CI job fails with broad CRAP regressions spanning unrelated functions, the first diagnostic step is to compare the PR's merge-base against origin/main to detect baseline drift rather than assuming a code regression.

The CI lcov artifact can be downloaded via GitHub Actions API (gh api repos/.../actions/jobs/.../logs and _download_workflow_artifact), extracted, path-normalized with perl -pi -e, and used to regenerate the baseline locally.
Polling CI with 'gh pr checks <number>' on sleep intervals (120s–360s) is the fallback when no event-driven notification is available; interval should grow as remaining jobs are the known-slow ones.

Reply to and resolve Codex/review threads explicitly after each fix push to keep the review state clean; use _reply_to_review_comment + _resolve_review_thread + _add_comment_to_issue to trigger a new Codex pass.

friction: Coverage CI job failed with misleading broad CRAP regressions caused by baseline drift from main advancing, not any change in the PR itself — required significant investigation to distinguish from a real regression.
CI lcov artifact uses absolute runner paths that break local CRAP tooling; required a perl path-rewrite step that is not documented in any visible repo tooling.
GitHub API log fetch hit a transient network error mid-session, requiring a retry.
Long CI polling loop with manual sleep intervals (up to 360s) due to lack of event-driven CI notification; several polling cycles returned no new information.
The 20k-line Coverage job log required targeted rg/sed extraction rather than direct reading.
open_items: No automated process exists to keep .crap-baseline.json in sync when main advances; teams must manually refresh it when CRAP fails on merge-ref CI — worth adding a bot or CI step to auto-update the baseline on main merges.
The lcov path-normalization step (runner paths → local paths) is undocumented and manual; could be wrapped in the xcrap tooling or a repo script.
Release Verification CI job status was not confirmed green in the transcript — final PR merge readiness depends on that job completing successfully.
